moneyline.market_read: OKC heavy favorite; market priced Thunder control but spread clustered around -7.5.

spread.notable_alt: Best Odds displayed SAS +8.5 / OKC -7.5 split

full_game_total.result: Over by roughly 18.5 to 20.5 points depending on book

positions_and_results.diagnosis: Spread protection failed; OKC crossed key live/closing threshold by 1.5 points.
Correct model read; Spurs pace/shot-volume and late scoring supported the over.

game_flow.q1: 31-31: neutral open, both offenses immediately ahead of conservative playoff total assumptions.
game_flow.q2: OKC 31-20: decisive Thunder separation phase; SAS turnover pressure and OKC shot-making created first major margin risk.
game_flow.q3: SAS 37-34: Spurs offense stayed alive and validated team-total over profile despite trailing.
game_flow.q4: OKC 26-25: OKC maintained enough scoring to cover; no backdoor compression arrived.

pace_and_scoring.finding: The game was not a slow playoff grind. Both teams cleared their team totals and the full-game total by a wide margin.
pace_and_scoring.patch_implication: Do not let playoff setting alone suppress totals when both teams show early rim-or-3 frequency and transition/live-ball turnover paths.

SAS.note: SAS generated a strong modern shot map: heavy rim + 3 blend, enough efficiency to clear team total comfortably.

OKC.midrange_pressure: OKC also produced 13/24 short-midrange and 7/16 long-midrange, giving them scoring redundancy beyond 3PA.
OKC.note: OKC did not need elite 3PT variance to clear. Their halfcourt shot creation and assisted 2PT flow carried the offense.

SAS.diagnosis: Primary leak. Spurs scoring still hit, but live-ball turnovers created OKC margin expansion and killed the +spread/no-OKC-cover angle.

OKC.diagnosis: OKC protected the ball well enough to avoid backdoor risk.

key_players.Victor Wembanyama: 17 rebounds on 27 chances; 10 contested rebounds; elite center-tree consolidation.
key_players.Isaiah Hartenstein: 13 rebounds on 18 chances; 7 contested; 6 box outs; dominant OKC glass stabilizer.
key_players.Jared McCain: 6 rebounds on 9 chances; guard rebound spike from long-rebound environment.

rebounding.diagnosis: Rebounding volume was high but not one-sided enough to rescue SAS spread. OKC offensive rebounding plus SAS turnovers supported OKC cover.

foul_context.finding: Foul counts were balanced; whistle did not create a one-sided team-total distortion. Both teams had 21 fouls drawn/committed profile, keeping scoring paths open.

hits: SAS team total over was directionally correct and should be retained as a ghost-bet validation hit.
Full-game over environment was visible through pace, shot map, and scoring distribution.
Wembanyama rebound ceiling remained real; 17 rebounds confirmed center-tree consolidation.
misses: Spread protection/no-OKC-cover failed because SAS turnover burden was not penalized enough.
OKC margin-extension risk was underweighted after halftime despite turnover gap and OKC offensive board stability.
Market split skepticism did not sufficiently override the live scoreboard/efficiency profile.
model_hit_miss_audit.root_cause: The model correctly read SAS scoring capacity but failed to separate team-total viability from spread viability. SAS could score enough while still giving away possessions that expanded OKC margin.

patches_to_activate.rule: A team-total over can be valid while spread/no-cover is invalid if live-ball turnovers exceed 12 or opponent offensive rebounds exceed 15.
If a trailing or underdog side has 14+ live-ball turnovers, downgrade all spread-protection positions by 8-14 percentage points.
When OKC posts 18+ OREB or an individual big exceeds 70% rebound chance conversion, raise OKC margin retention probability.
SAS TT over 102.5 was correct and should be retained as a model-positive but non-wager/ghost result.
No-cover positions must be stress-tested against late free throws, offensive rebounding, and live-ball turnover margin before entry.

simulation_ready_notes.postgame_calibration: Actual 235 landed above mean because both teams sustained scoring while SAS turnovers increased OKC possession quality.
